categories.title: LIGHTS, CAMERA, ACTION!
categories.theme: Film clues about iconic movies and the actors who starred in them — answer is the FILM TITLE or ACTOR NAME as specified

clues.clue: Harrison Ford cracked his whip in this 1981 adventure classic, playing an archaeologist chasing a biblical artifact.
Jack Nicholson descended into madness at the Overlook Hotel in this 1980 Stanley Kubrick horror film.
This 1994 Oliver Stone film features the infamous quote 'We rob banks,' spoken by its outlaw title characters.
Steve Buscemi played the color-coded alias Mr. Pink in this Quentin Tarantino debut about a heist gone wrong.
This actor, who appeared in both Pulp Fiction and The Incredible Hulk, also played General Thaddeus Ross's adversary onscreen.

categories.title: LIGHTS, CAMERA, OSCAR!
categories.theme: Academy Award acting wins tied to specific roles — answer is the ACTOR OR ACTRESS who won

clues.clue: He took home the Best Actor statue in 1994 for his portrayal of a slow-witted but big-hearted Alabaman running through American history.
This actress earned Best Actress honors in 2018 for her cunning portrayal of Queen Anne in the darkly comic period film 'The Favourite.'
Her role as the emotionally volatile Tiffany in 'Silver Linings Playbook' earned this actress the Best Actress Oscar in 2012.
This veteran British actor won Best Actor in 2017 for transforming into a wartime prime minister in 'Darkest Hour.'
This film about identity, sexuality, and growing up in Miami took the Best Picture prize in 2016 — name the film, not the actors.

finals.title: BEHIND THE CAMERA
finals.clue: Before becoming synonymous with talking aliens and sharp suits, this director helmed Men in Black in 1997 — a fact often overshadowed by the film's stars Will Smith and Tommy Lee Jones.

finals.title: BOND SONGS
finals.clue: This British new-wave band, better known for 'Hungry Like the Wolf,' performed the title track for the 1985 James Bond film set partly in Paris and San Francisco.
